The tongue is a crucial organ in mechanical digestion and taste. Taste buds contain taste receptor cells which are the smallest functional unit in gustation. Taste buds can be found throughout the length of the upper digestive tract. On the surface of the tongue are protrusions called papillae. Circumvallate papillae are arranged in a v shape pattern on toward the base of the tongue, on the dorsal aspect, and contain more than 100 taste buds each.
